# Design – Device Tracker

---

## Domain Model

Designed in Visual Paradigm Online. The model contains 6 classes: **Device** (central entity with Id, Name, Type, Status, SystemName, Location, IpAddress, AssignedTo, timestamps), **Issue** (linked to Device via DeviceId, tracks problems with severity and resolution), **Assignment** (links a technician to a device task), and three enums: **DeviceType**, **DeviceStatus**, and **IssueSeverity**. Device has a one-to-many relationship with both Issue and Assignment.

![Domain Model](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

---

## Activity Diagrams

One activity diagram per use case, showing the step-by-step flow through the console application.

![Activity Diagrams](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**UC-1 Add Device:** User navigates to Manage Devices → Add → enters name, selects type, enters system/location/IP → device saved with Pending status.

**UC-2 Update Status:** User enters device ID → system validates → shows current status → user selects new status → saved with updated timestamp.

**UC-3 Log Issue:** User enters device ID → enters description → selects severity → enters reporter name → issue linked to device and saved.

**UC-4 Assign Device:** User enters device ID and technician name → enters task → device AssignedTo updated → Assignment record created.

**UC-5 Filter Devices:** User optionally filters by status, type, system name, technician → system applies partial case-insensitive filters → results displayed.

**UC-6 Generate Report:** System counts devices by status and system → lists open issues → displays formatted report → optional file export.

---

## UI Design

Console screen mockups showing all key application screens with color-coded status indicators.

![UI Design](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The UI uses color coding throughout: **green** for Active devices, **yellow** for Pending, **red** for Faulty, **cyan** for Installed. All navigation uses numbered menus. Prompts show expected input format. Success messages use ✓ prefix, errors use ✗.

---

## Prototype

Annotated console screen sketches showing the complete user flow from main menu through all major features.

![Prototype](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The prototype covers five screens: ① Main Menu, ② Device Sub-Menu, ③ Device List View with color-coded statuses, ④ Issue Log with severity coloring, and ⑤ Status Report with export option.

---

## Architecture

Designed in Excalidraw. The application follows a strict 4-layer architecture.

![Architecture](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**Key architectural decisions:**
- **Flat JSON storage** — no database required; data persists to `~/AppData/DeviceTracker/` as three JSON files (`devices.json`, `issues.json`, `assignments.json`)
- **Shared DataStore instance** — all services operate on the same DataStore object so mutations in one service are immediately visible to others without coordination
- **ConsoleHelper abstraction** — all console output is routed through ConsoleHelper, fully decoupling display formatting from business logic
- **Constructor injection** — DataStore is passed to each service via constructor, making unit testing straightforward with isolated temp directories
